questions.question_text:
According to the original text of the US Constitution, written in 1787, the presidential candidate receiving the
second-most Electoral College votes becomes vice president. The 12th Amendment, ratified in
_ separated the
elections for the two offices.
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.study_category_label: Standard English Conventions: punctuation, titles, and modifier placement

answer_explanation.why_correct: The phrase "ratified in 1804" is a nonessential modifier interrupting the main clause "The 12th Amendment ... separated the elections." It needs a comma after 1804 to close that interrupting phrase.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice D leaves the modifier without a closing comma, so the sentence is not punctuated correctly. The year is not part of the core subject.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When an interrupting phrase can be removed without changing the sentence's core grammar, it should usually be set off with matching punctuation on both sides.

questions.question_text:
The Panama Canal, completed in ___ connected the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans by way of Central America.
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.study_category_label: Standard English Conventions: punctuation, titles, and modifier placement

answer_explanation.why_correct: "Completed in 1914" is a nonessential modifier interrupting the sentence, so it should be closed with a comma after the year.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice D leaves the interrupting phrase without closing punctuation.
answer_explanation.takeaway: If a date appears inside a removable modifier, use matching punctuation to set that modifier off from the main clause.

questions.question_text:
The city library, renovated in ___ reopened with expanded reading rooms and archival space.
Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.study_category_label: Standard English Conventions: punctuation, titles, and modifier placement

answer_explanation.why_correct: The phrase "renovated in 2008" interrupts the core clause "The city library reopened," so a comma is needed after 2008.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Without the comma in choice D, the nonessential modifier is not properly set off.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Find the core subject and verb first; then punctuate any interrupting date phrase around that core.

questions.question_text:
Long thought to be sessile (immobile), adult Chelonibia testudinaria, barnacles that adhere to sea turtle shells, have
been observed to shift slightly in position over time — a phenomenon that has been attributed to the barnacles'
passive displacement by water currents. _
_ a research team found that adult C. testudinaria moved toward the
heads of their sea turtle hosts and thus against the prevailing water flow, behavior consistent with self-initiated
locomotion.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?

choices.A: Contrary to this phenomenon,
choices.B: Undermining this explanation,
choices.C: Drawing a similar conclusion,
choices.D: Confirming this hypothesis,

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Contrary to this phenomenon,

correct_answer.text: Undermining this explanation,

questions.study_category_label: Expression of Ideas: transitions and notes-to-sentence synthesis

answer_explanation.why_correct: The first sentence gives an explanation for the barnacles' movement: passive displacement by water currents. The next sentence reports movement against the water flow, so it weakens that explanation rather than contradicting the movement itself.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A points to the wrong noun. The new evidence is not contrary to the phenomenon of shifting position; it is contrary to the explanation that currents caused the shifting.
answer_explanation.takeaway: For transitions, identify whether the next sentence supports or undercuts the previous claim, and be precise about which claim it is responding to.

questions.question_text:
Long assumed to drift only because of changing currents, juvenile mangrove seeds have been observed to alter their direction over time - a pattern often explained as passive displacement by tidal flow. ___ a field team found that the seeds sometimes turned into sheltered eddies and thus away from the strongest current, behavior consistent with shape-based steering rather than simple drift.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?

choices.A: Contrary to this phenomenon,
choices.B: Undermining this explanation,
choices.C: Drawing a similar conclusion,
choices.D: Confirming this hypothesis,

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Contrary to this phenomenon,

correct_answer.text: Undermining this explanation,

questions.study_category_label: Expression of Ideas: transitions and notes-to-sentence synthesis

answer_explanation.why_correct: The second sentence weakens the explanation that currents alone caused the movement. "Undermining this explanation" describes that relationship exactly.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A says the new evidence is contrary to the movement itself, but the movement still occurs. What changes is the explanation for it.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Be precise about what the next sentence is challenging: the phenomenon or the explanation of the phenomenon.

questions.question_text:
For years, scientists assumed that certain sand dollars shift position on the seafloor only because of wave action, and the animals' slow movement was attributed to passive rolling by water. ___ divers observed the sand dollars creeping toward patches of algae and sometimes moving against the direction of the current, behavior more consistent with self-propelled motion.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?

choices.A: Contrary to this phenomenon,
choices.B: Undermining this explanation,
choices.C: Drawing a similar conclusion,
choices.D: Confirming this hypothesis,

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Contrary to this phenomenon,

correct_answer.text: Undermining this explanation,

questions.study_category_label: Expression of Ideas: transitions and notes-to-sentence synthesis

answer_explanation.why_correct: The diver observations call the current-based explanation into question. The motion remains real, but the explanation is weakened.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A misidentifies the target of the contrast. The new evidence is not contrary to the sand dollars moving; it is contrary to the idea that water alone moves them.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Transition questions often hinge on the noun after the transition. Make sure it points to the right prior idea.

questions.question_text:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
• Edmonia Lewis (1844-1907) was an African American and Mississauga Ojibwe sculptor.
• Forever Free (1867) is a marble sculpture by Lewis.
• It depicts a male figure and a female figure gazing upward.
• It commemorates the 1863 Emancipation Proclamation.
• The phrase "forever free" from the text of the Emancipation Proclamation is inscribed on the sculpture's
base.
• Art historian Kirsten Buick describes the sculpture as a "celebration of liberty."
The student wants to explain the sculpture's specific historical context. Which choice most effectively uses relevant
information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.A: According to art historian Kirsten Buick, Forever Free, which depicts a male figure and a female figure gazing upward, is a "celebration of liberty."
choices.B: The base of Edmonia Lewis's 1867 marble sculpture is inscribed with a historically significant phrase: "forever free."
choices.C: Completed in 1867, Lewis's sculpture Forever Free commemorates the Emancipation Proclamation, which had been issued four years previously.
choices.D: Forever Free (1867) is a marble sculpture by Edmonia Lewis, an African American and Mississauga Ojibwe sculptor who lived from 1844 to 1907.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: According to art historian Kirsten Buick, Forever Free, which depicts a male figure and a female figure gazing upward, is a "celebration of liberty."

correct_answer.text: Completed in 1867, Lewis's sculpture Forever Free commemorates the Emancipation Proclamation, which had been issued four years previously.

questions.study_category_label: Expression of Ideas: transitions and notes-to-sentence synthesis

answer_explanation.why_correct: The goal is to explain the sculpture's specific historical context, so the best answer should connect the work to a historical event and time frame. Choice C does that by linking the 1867 sculpture to the 1863 Emancipation Proclamation.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A gives interpretation and visual description, but it does not place the sculpture in its historical moment. The question asks for context, not praise or imagery.
answer_explanation.takeaway: In notes-to-sentence questions, match the answer to the stated writing goal, not just to facts that are true.

questions.question_text:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
- Diego Rivera completed the mural cycle Detroit Industry in 1933.
- The murals are housed at the Detroit Institute of Arts.
- They depict factory workers and industrial machines.
- The murals were created during the Great Depression.
- Rivera was interested in labor and public art.
- Many critics praise the murals' scale and energy.
The student wants to explain the murals' specific historical context. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.A: Critics often praise Detroit Industry for its scale and energy, and the murals show workers operating large industrial machines.
choices.B: Detroit Industry, a mural cycle by Diego Rivera, is housed at the Detroit Institute of Arts and reflects Rivera's interest in public art.
choices.C: Completed in 1933, Rivera's Detroit Industry murals were created during the Great Depression, when industrial labor was central to public debate.
choices.D: Diego Rivera, who cared deeply about labor, finished Detroit Industry in 1933 and filled the murals with images of factory workers.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Critics often praise Detroit Industry for its scale and energy, and the murals show workers operating large industrial machines.

correct_answer.text: Completed in 1933, Rivera's Detroit Industry murals were created during the Great Depression, when industrial labor was central to public debate.

questions.study_category_label: Expression of Ideas: transitions and notes-to-sentence synthesis

answer_explanation.why_correct: Historical context means placing the work in relation to a specific time and event or era. Choice C does that by linking the 1933 murals to the Great Depression.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A offers description and critical reaction, but it does not situate the murals in a historical moment.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When the goal is historical context, the best answer usually ties the work to a dated event, movement, or era.

questions.question_text:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
- Dorothea Lange took Migrant Mother in 1936.
- The photograph shows a mother with children leaning against her.
- It became one of the best-known images of the Great Depression.
- Many farm families faced severe hardship during the Dust Bowl and Depression.
- Lange worked for the Farm Security Administration.
- Critics often describe the image as emotionally powerful.
The student wants to explain the photograph's specific historical context. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

choices.A: Critics often describe Migrant Mother as emotionally powerful, and the photograph shows a mother with children leaning against her.
choices.B: Taken by Farm Security Administration photographer Dorothea Lange, Migrant Mother became one of the best-known photographs in US history.
choices.C: Taken in 1936, Migrant Mother emerged during the Great Depression, when many farm families were struggling through economic crisis and Dust Bowl conditions.
choices.D: Dorothea Lange's 1936 photograph Migrant Mother shows a mother with children leaning against her and has become widely recognized.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Critics often describe Migrant Mother as emotionally powerful, and the photograph shows a mother with children leaning against her.

correct_answer.text: Taken in 1936, Migrant Mother emerged during the Great Depression, when many farm families were struggling through economic crisis and Dust Bowl conditions.

questions.study_category_label: Expression of Ideas: transitions and notes-to-sentence synthesis

answer_explanation.why_correct: Choice C connects the photograph to the Great Depression and Dust Bowl hardship, which is exactly the kind of historical background the student wants.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A stays with description and evaluation rather than historical context.
answer_explanation.takeaway: For notes synthesis, discard any choice that is true but does not perform the exact writing task named in the prompt.

questions.question_text:
Scholars long thought that the initial spread of silk beyond China occurred in the second century CE, but this view
has been _
by new archaeological evidence from South Asia that reveals that the people of the Indus
Civilization made use of silk at least 1,000 years earlier.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: investigated
choices.B: misinterpreted
choices.C: anticipated
choices.D: contradicted

selected_incorrect_answer.text: investigated

correct_answer.text: contradicted

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: The new archaeological evidence shows the old timeline was wrong by at least 1,000 years. "Contradicted" is the precise word for evidence that conflicts with and overturns an earlier view.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: "Investigated" only means examined or studied. The sentence needs a word showing that the evidence conflicts with the old claim, not merely that scholars looked into it.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When new evidence changes an earlier belief, look for a verb that captures the relationship between the evidence and the prior claim.

questions.question_text:
Historians once believed that glassmaking reached northern Europe only in the first century CE, but excavations at several Baltic settlements have ___ that view by uncovering evidence of local glass production centuries earlier.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: investigated
choices.B: misread
choices.C: anticipated
choices.D: contradicted

selected_incorrect_answer.text: investigated

correct_answer.text: contradicted

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: The new evidence shows the earlier view was wrong, so the precise verb is "contradicted."
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: "Investigated" only means examined. The sentence needs a word showing conflict with the old view.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When evidence overturns an earlier belief, choose the verb that states that conflict directly.

questions.question_text:
Astronomers had long assumed that exoplanet K2-117 lacked any substantial atmosphere, but recent spectral data have ___ that assumption by revealing the presence of water vapor around the planet.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: investigated
choices.B: misinterpreted
choices.C: anticipated
choices.D: contradicted

selected_incorrect_answer.text: investigated

correct_answer.text: contradicted

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: Detecting water vapor conflicts with the claim that the planet lacks an atmosphere, so "contradicted" is the best fit.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: "Investigated" does not show that the evidence disproved the assumption.
answer_explanation.takeaway: A new finding that proves an old claim false calls for a strong contradiction word, not a neutral research word.

questions.question_text:
Archaeologists have identified a plethora of beads fashioned from Tritia gibbosula shells in many Middle Stone Age
(MSA) sites across western North Africa, including El Mnasra Cave, Morocco. In a 2021 paper, El Mehdi Sehasseh
et al. attribute these artifacts' apparent _
_ to the evolution and propagation of symbolic behavior (e.g., use of
personal ornaments) in humans and the widespread availability of T. gibbosula during the MSA.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: heterogeneity
choices.B: ubiquity
choices.C: expediency
choices.D: fecundity

selected_incorrect_answer.text: heterogeneity

correct_answer.text: ubiquity

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: The passage says beads made from these shells were found in many MSA sites across western North Africa. "Ubiquity" means widespread presence, which matches that description exactly.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: "Heterogeneity" means diversity or variety, but the sentence is about how common the artifacts were across many sites, not how different they were from one another.
answer_explanation.takeaway: A strong vocabulary answer should fit both the surrounding logic and the exact noun being described.

questions.question_text:
Archaeologists have recovered obsidian blades from dozens of settlements across a broad volcanic region. In a recent paper, researchers attribute these tools' apparent ___ to long-distance trade networks and the ready availability of obsidian outcrops in the area.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: heterogeneity
choices.B: ubiquity
choices.C: expediency
choices.D: fecundity

selected_incorrect_answer.text: heterogeneity

correct_answer.text: ubiquity

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: Because the blades were found in many settlements, the sentence calls for a word meaning widespread presence. "Ubiquity" fits exactly.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: "Heterogeneity" means diversity or variation, but the passage is about how common the tools are across sites.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Match the noun to the evidence: "many sites" points to widespread occurrence, not diversity.

questions.question_text:
Marine archaeologists have identified the same style of bronze coin at ports throughout the eastern Mediterranean. A 2024 study attributes the coins' apparent ___ to dense shipping routes and the coin's use in multiple coastal markets.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: heterogeneity
choices.B: ubiquity
choices.C: expediency
choices.D: fecundity

selected_incorrect_answer.text: heterogeneity

correct_answer.text: ubiquity

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: Finding the same coin style at many ports shows that it was widespread, or ubiquitous.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: The study is not about the coins being diverse; it is about their being found almost everywhere in the region.
answer_explanation.takeaway: If the clue is geographic spread, the right answer will usually mean prevalence, not difference.

questions.question_text:
The familial structure of the black swan (Cygnus atratus) shows tremendous
_: breeding pairs with
demonstrated success in rearing cygnets (young swans) have been observed to foster those of less successful pairs,
and while rearing by female-male pairs is most common, male-male pairs sometimes rear cygnets too, achieving a
significantly higher rate of survival to adulthood than female-male pairs.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: cohesion
choices.B: elasticity
choices.C: affection
choices.D: reciprocity

selected_incorrect_answer.text: affection

correct_answer.text: elasticity

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: The examples show that black swan family arrangements are flexible: successful pairs foster others' cygnets, and male-male pairs can also rear young. "Elasticity" best captures that adaptability or range.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: "Affection" points to emotion, but the passage is describing variation in family structure and caregiving patterns, not how loving the swans are.
answer_explanation.takeaway: If the examples show range or flexibility, choose the option that captures structure and pattern rather than tone or feeling.

questions.question_text:
The social organization of clownfish shows remarkable ___: if the dominant female in a group disappears, the breeding male can change sex and assume that role, and unrelated juveniles may also be absorbed into the group's hierarchy.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: cohesion
choices.B: elasticity
choices.C: affection
choices.D: reciprocity

selected_incorrect_answer.text: affection

correct_answer.text: elasticity

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: The examples show flexibility and adaptability in the group structure, so "elasticity" is the most precise word.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: "Affection" refers to emotion, but the passage is about how the social structure can change shape.
answer_explanation.takeaway: If the examples show a system bending or adapting, look for a structural word rather than an emotional one.

questions.question_text:
The care structure of acorn woodpeckers exhibits notable ___: breeding pairs are often assisted by helpers, and adults may join or leave cooperative groups while the nest still functions successfully.
Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

choices.A: cohesion
choices.B: elasticity
choices.C: affection
choices.D: reciprocity

selected_incorrect_answer.text: affection

correct_answer.text: elasticity

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: word-in-context and precision vocabulary

answer_explanation.why_correct: The family arrangement can shift while remaining effective, so the passage is describing flexibility, or elasticity.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: The evidence does not show how fond the birds are of one another; it shows that the caregiving arrangement is adaptable.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Abstract nouns on SAT items often test whether you can separate relationship structure from emotional tone.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from Bram Stoker's 1911 novel The Lair of the White Worm. Adam is meeting his great-uncle
Richard at a port.
The meeting so auspiciously begun proceeded well. Adam, seeing that the old man was interested in the
novelty of the ship, suggested that he should stay the night on board, and that he would himself be ready to
start at any hour and go anywhere that the other suggested. This affectionate willingness to fall in with his
own plans quite won the old man's heart. He warmly accepted the invitation, and at once they became not
only on terms of affectionate relationship, but almost like old friends.
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

choices.A: It states the reasons why Adam and his great-uncle Richard decide to sleep on the ship rather than finding lodging on land.
choices.B: It showcases how Adam's flexibility and consideration strengthen his relationship with his great-uncle Richard.
choices.C: It describes why Adam and his great-uncle Richard are excited for their upcoming journey on the ship.
choices.D: It contrasts great-uncle Richard's wary first impressions of Adam with his ultimate affection toward him.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: It contrasts great-uncle Richard's wary first impressions of Adam with his ultimate affection toward him.

correct_answer.text: It showcases how Adam's flexibility and consideration strengthen his relationship with his great-uncle Richard.

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: passage structure, sentence function, and author purpose

answer_explanation.why_correct: The passage centers on Adam's considerate offer to stay on the ship and be ready to follow Richard's plans, then shows that this wins Richard's heart. Choice B captures that Adam's flexibility improves their relationship.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice D depends on a contrast the text never gives. Richard's first impression is not described as wary; the meeting is said to have begun auspiciously and proceeded well.
answer_explanation.takeaway: For main-purpose questions, focus on the change the passage highlights, not on a dramatic contrast that is never actually stated.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from a novel. Nora is meeting her aunt Lydia at a train station.
The reunion, happily begun, improved with every moment. Noticing that Lydia was tired from travel, Nora insisted that they abandon her own plans for the afternoon and settle first wherever Lydia pleased. This ready willingness to adjust herself to another's comfort affected Lydia deeply. She took Nora's arm at once, and before long the two were speaking not stiffly as relatives, but with the ease of old companions.
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

choices.A: It explains why Nora and her aunt Lydia decide to spend the afternoon together rather than continuing their separate plans.
choices.B: It showcases how Nora's flexibility and consideration strengthen her relationship with her aunt Lydia.
choices.C: It describes why Nora and her aunt Lydia feel enthusiastic about the errands ahead of them.
choices.D: It contrasts Lydia's wary first impression of Nora with her eventual affection for Nora.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: It contrasts Lydia's wary first impression of Nora with her eventual affection for Nora.

correct_answer.text: It showcases how Nora's flexibility and consideration strengthen her relationship with her aunt Lydia.

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: passage structure, sentence function, and author purpose

answer_explanation.why_correct: The passage emphasizes Nora's willingness to adapt to Lydia's needs and shows that this warms their relationship immediately. Choice B states that purpose directly.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: The passage never says Lydia was wary at first. It begins by saying the reunion was happily begun.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Do not choose a contrast answer unless the passage clearly presents both sides of the contrast.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from a novel. Julian has just met the retired teacher Mr. Vale, whom he has long admired.
Their conversation opened well and went on better. Seeing that Mr. Vale wished to walk slowly and talk at leisure, Julian at once gave up the quicker route he had planned and matched his pace to the older man's. Such easy deference to another's comfort won Mr. Vale completely. By the time they reached the garden gate, they had ceased to seem like strangers and spoken instead with the warmth of old friends.
Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?

choices.A: It states the reasons Julian and Mr. Vale choose the slower route to the garden rather than the quicker one.
choices.B: It showcases how Julian's flexibility and consideration strengthen his relationship with Mr. Vale.
choices.C: It describes why Julian and Mr. Vale are excited about the conversation they plan to continue in the garden.
choices.D: It contrasts Mr. Vale's suspicious first impression of Julian with his eventual fondness for him.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: It contrasts Mr. Vale's suspicious first impression of Julian with his eventual fondness for him.

correct_answer.text: It showcases how Julian's flexibility and consideration strengthen his relationship with Mr. Vale.

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: passage structure, sentence function, and author purpose

answer_explanation.why_correct: Julian alters his own plan out of consideration, and the passage shows that this creates immediate closeness between the two characters.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice D invents suspicion that the text never mentions. The relationship starts positively and grows warmer.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Main-purpose answers should capture the change the passage highlights, not an invented backstory.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from William Shakespeare's circa 1611 play The Winter's Tale. Camillo has been away fron
his home in Sicily and serves in the court of Polixenes, the king of Bohemia. He has asked Polixenes for permission
to return to Sicily.
POLIXENES: I pray thee, good Camillo, be no more
importunate. 'Tis a sickness denying thee anything,
a death to grant this.
CAMILLO: It is fifteen years since I saw my country.
Though I have for the most part been aired abroad,
I desire to lay my bones there. Besides, the penitent
king, my master, hath sent for me, to whose feeling
sorrows I might be some allay-or I o'erween (presume to
think so — which is another spur to my departure.
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

choices.A: It establishes Camillo's hope that Polixenes will be comforted knowing that Camillo isn't departing at his own discretion.
choices.B: It suggests that Camillo feels compelled to persuade Polixenes that Camillo's decision to leave the court is justified.
choices.C: It conveys Camillo's recognition that one benefit his presence may afford the king of Sicily is merely speculative.
choices.D: It bolsters the idea that Camillo's primary motivation to return home is his concern for the king of Sicily's well-being in his absence.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: It establishes Camillo's hope that Polixenes will be comforted knowing that Camillo isn't departing at his own discretion.

correct_answer.text: It conveys Camillo's recognition that one benefit his presence may afford the king of Sicily is merely speculative.

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: passage structure, sentence function, and author purpose

answer_explanation.why_correct: Camillo says he might help ease the Sicilian king's sorrows, then immediately checks himself with "or I o'erween," meaning he may be presuming too much. That aside shows he knows this possible benefit is uncertain or speculative.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A shifts the focus to Polixenes's feelings, but the underlined aside is about Camillo's own doubt regarding how useful he would be to the king of Sicily.
answer_explanation.takeaway: In older literary passages, brief self-corrections often signal qualification: the speaker is softening or limiting what he just claimed.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from a play. Marta has asked the duke for permission to leave court and return home.
DUKE: I beg you, Marta, press me no further. To refuse you pains me, yet to let you go pains me more.
MARTA: It is ten years since I saw my sister. Besides, our grieving lady has written for me, whose burdened heart I may perhaps lighten - or I presume too much - and that hope urges me to depart.
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

choices.A: It establishes Marta's hope that the duke will be comforted knowing that Marta is not leaving merely by choice.
choices.B: It suggests that Marta feels compelled to persuade the duke that her decision to leave court is justified.
choices.C: It conveys Marta's recognition that one benefit her presence may afford the grieving lady is merely speculative.
choices.D: It bolsters the idea that Marta's primary motivation for returning home is concern for the grieving lady's well-being.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: It establishes Marta's hope that the duke will be comforted knowing that Marta is not leaving merely by choice.

correct_answer.text: It conveys Marta's recognition that one benefit her presence may afford the grieving lady is merely speculative.

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: passage structure, sentence function, and author purpose

answer_explanation.why_correct: Marta says she may lighten the lady's burden, then immediately qualifies that thought with "or I presume too much." That shows she knows the benefit is uncertain.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: The aside is not about comforting the duke. It is about Marta doubting her own usefulness.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When a speaker interrupts herself with a phrase like "or I presume too much," she is qualifying the certainty of what she just claimed.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from a play. An envoy has asked the queen for leave to travel home.
QUEEN: Good Rowan, importune me no more. It troubles me to deny you and troubles me no less to grant this request.
ROWAN: Twelve winters have passed since I saw my father. And the ailing prince has sent for me, to whose anxieties I might be some relief - or I flatter myself - which thought also presses me toward departure.
Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

choices.A: It establishes Rowan's hope that the queen will feel less slighted if she knows Rowan is not leaving entirely of his own accord.
choices.B: It suggests that Rowan feels compelled to persuade the queen that Rowan's decision to leave is justified.
choices.C: It conveys Rowan's recognition that one benefit Rowan's presence may afford the prince is merely speculative.
choices.D: It bolsters the idea that Rowan's primary motivation for returning home is concern for the prince's well-being.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: It establishes Rowan's hope that the queen will feel less slighted if she knows Rowan is not leaving entirely of his own accord.

correct_answer.text: It conveys Rowan's recognition that one benefit Rowan's presence may afford the prince is merely speculative.

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: passage structure, sentence function, and author purpose

answer_explanation.why_correct: The phrase "or I flatter myself" shows Rowan checking his own confidence about being able to help the prince. The possible benefit is presented as uncertain.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A wrongly shifts attention to the queen's feelings. Rowan's aside comments on his own doubtful estimate of his usefulness.
answer_explanation.takeaway: A self-correcting aside usually narrows or softens the claim immediately before it.

questions.question_text:
Text 1
Chemically analyzing bulk rock-core samples from Australia's Mount McRae Shale, Ariel Anbar et al. found a
transient but significant increase in molybdenum (Mo) at a point corresponding to roughly 2.5 billion years ago (Ga).
On Earth, Mo is released mainly through oxidative weathering of minerals; Anbar et al. therefore concluded that
atmospheric oxygen briefly increased 2.5 Ga, then returned to its earlier negligible level.
Text 2
Sarah Slotznick et al. reexamined the Mount McRae Shale core. Since chemically analyzing bulk samples can
occlude contextual details, Slotznick et al. also employed high-resolution microscopy, which revealed volcanic
debris— a known Mo host-around 2.5 Ga and microfractures in the surrounding matrix. The researchers assert that
fluid could have reached the debris through the microfractures and initiated oxidative weathering long after debris
deposition.
Based on the texts, Anbar et al. (Text 1) and Slotznick et al. (Text 2) would most likely disagree about the answer to
which question about the portion of the Mount McRae Shale rock-core corresponding to 2.5 Ga?

choices.A: Does the increase in Mo in that portion suggest that atmospheric oxygen levels were not negligible before 2.5 Ga?
choices.B: Is the increase in Mo in that portion indicative of an increase in atmospheric oxygen dating to the same time?
choices.C: Is the increase in Mo in that portion attributable to oxidative weathering of the material found in that portion?
choices.D: Did chemically analyzing bulk samples lead to a false impression that there is an increase in Mo in that portion?

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Does the increase in Mo in that portion suggest that atmospheric oxygen levels were not negligible before 2.5 Ga?

correct_answer.text: Is the increase in Mo in that portion indicative of an increase in atmospheric oxygen dating to the same time?

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: paired-text response and disagreement

answer_explanation.why_correct: Anbar et al. treat the Mo spike as evidence of an oxygen increase at 2.5 Ga, while Slotznick et al. argue the oxidative weathering could have happened much later. So the real disagreement is whether the Mo increase indicates an oxygen increase dating to the same time as that rock layer.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A asks about oxygen levels before 2.5 Ga, which neither text addresses. The dispute is about the timing of the oxygen-related weathering, not about an earlier baseline.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Turn paired-text disagreements into a yes-or-no timing or causation question; the right answer is the one the two texts would answer differently.

questions.question_text:
Text 1
By chemically analyzing lake-sediment cores from a boreal forest, Lena Ortiz et al. found a sharp spike in charcoal fragments in a layer corresponding to roughly 8,000 years ago. Because charcoal is produced by fire, Ortiz et al. concluded that the region experienced an intense wildfire at that time.
Text 2
Hao Chen et al. reexamined the same cores with high-resolution imaging. In addition to the charcoal-rich layer, Chen et al. found root channels and tiny cracks extending downward from higher layers. The researchers argue that later groundwater could have carried charcoal into the older layer long after the surrounding sediment was deposited.
Based on the texts, Ortiz et al. (Text 1) and Chen et al. (Text 2) would most likely disagree about the answer to which question about the sediment layer corresponding to 8,000 years ago?

choices.A: Does the increase in charcoal fragments in that layer suggest that wildfire activity was already high before 8,000 years ago?
choices.B: Is the increase in charcoal fragments in that layer indicative of a wildfire dating to the same time?
choices.C: Is the increase in charcoal fragments in that layer attributable to burning material found in that layer?
choices.D: Did chemically analyzing bulk samples lead to a false impression that there is an increase in charcoal fragments in that layer?

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Does the increase in charcoal fragments in that layer suggest that wildfire activity was already high before 8,000 years ago?

correct_answer.text: Is the increase in charcoal fragments in that layer indicative of a wildfire dating to the same time?

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: paired-text response and disagreement

answer_explanation.why_correct: Text 1 says the charcoal spike reflects a wildfire at the time the layer formed, whereas Text 2 says the charcoal may have entered the layer later. Their disagreement is about whether the spike dates to that same time.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Neither text addresses wildfire activity before 8,000 years ago, so choice A misses the actual dispute.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When two texts disagree about evidence in a layer or sample, the key issue is often whether the evidence is contemporaneous with that layer.

questions.question_text:
Text 1
Analyzing mineral deposits in a cave wall, Sofia Malik et al. detected an unusual sulfur spike in material corresponding to roughly 12,000 years ago. Because sulfur deposits of this kind are often associated with volcanic emissions, Malik et al. concluded that volcanic activity briefly increased at that time.
Text 2
Reexamining the wall with microscopic imaging, Andre Vogel et al. identified later seepage channels running through the same area. Vogel et al. argue that sulfur-rich water could have entered the older material long after its formation, concentrating sulfur there without any volcanic increase at 12,000 years ago.
Based on the texts, Malik et al. (Text 1) and Vogel et al. (Text 2) would most likely disagree about the answer to which question about the cave-wall material corresponding to 12,000 years ago?

choices.A: Does the sulfur spike in that material suggest that volcanic activity was already elevated before 12,000 years ago?
choices.B: Is the sulfur spike in that material indicative of a volcanic increase dating to the same time?
choices.C: Is the sulfur spike in that material attributable to minerals present in that material?
choices.D: Did chemically analyzing the material create a false impression that sulfur levels were elevated there at all?

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Does the sulfur spike in that material suggest that volcanic activity was already elevated before 12,000 years ago?

correct_answer.text: Is the sulfur spike in that material indicative of a volcanic increase dating to the same time?

questions.study_category_label: Craft and Structure: paired-text response and disagreement

answer_explanation.why_correct: Malik et al. connect the sulfur spike to volcanic activity at 12,000 years ago, while Vogel et al. say the sulfur may have arrived later. The disagreement is about same-time interpretation.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A asks about an earlier baseline that neither text discusses.
answer_explanation.takeaway: If one text argues later contamination or seepage, the disagreement is about timing, not about what happened before the measured layer.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from Mick Herron's 2023 novel The Secret Hours. The narrator is describing members of a
government committee.
Finally, and adding much-needed gravitas, was Sir Winston Day, whose features seemed moulded to adorn a
bust, or possibly a stamp, and whose forehead was so evidently bulging with grey matter that it would have
been impertinent to inquire too closely into the actual achievements his half century of public service had
produced. His recently published memoirs possibly cast light on this enigma, but given that such details were
not provided until after the thirty-page mark, they might as well have remained state secrets.
©2023 by Mick Herron
Based on the text, which choice best describes Sir Winston Day?

choices.A: He has the appearance of a distinguished figure, but it is uncertain whether he has accomplished anything to earn distinction.
choices.B: He looks like a person worthy of respect, but his memoirs reveal that some of his actions were dishonorable.
choices.C: He would be a celebrated public figure if his achievements did not have to be kept secret.
choices.D: He has maintained a modest profile even though he has served the public capably for many years.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: He would be a celebrated public figure if his achievements did not have to be kept secret.

correct_answer.text: He has the appearance of a distinguished figure, but it is uncertain whether he has accomplished anything to earn distinction.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: The narrator describes Sir Winston Day as looking like a statesman, but then sarcastically questions what he actually achieved in fifty years of service. Choice A captures that contrast between impressive appearance and doubtful accomplishment.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice C treats "state secrets" literally, but the passage uses that phrase sarcastically to say the memoirs do not really clarify his achievements. The problem is vagueness, not classified heroism.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When the narrator is ironic, prefer the answer that matches the attitude beneath the praise rather than the praise on the surface.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from a novel. The narrator is describing a member of a civic board.
Last, and determined to look indispensable, was Councillor Beech, whose profile appeared designed for a commemorative medallion and whose brow suggested such reserves of judgment that it felt rude to ask what, exactly, three decades on the planning committee had achieved. His memoir might have answered that question, but because the relevant chapter begins so late and says so little, it may as well have been sealed in the town archives.
Based on the text, which choice best describes Councillor Beech?

choices.A: He has the appearance of a distinguished figure, but it is uncertain whether he has accomplished anything to earn distinction.
choices.B: He looks like a person worthy of respect, but his memoir reveals that some of his actions were dishonorable.
choices.C: He would be a celebrated public figure if his achievements did not have to be kept hidden from the public.
choices.D: He has maintained a modest profile even though he has served the public capably for many years.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: He would be a celebrated public figure if his achievements did not have to be kept hidden from the public.

correct_answer.text: He has the appearance of a distinguished figure, but it is uncertain whether he has accomplished anything to earn distinction.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: The narrator mockingly describes Beech as looking impressive while doubting that he has done anything important. Choice A captures that irony.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice C takes the archival joke literally. The narrator is not saying Beech's achievements are secret; the narrator is saying the memoir fails to show any.
answer_explanation.takeaway: In ironic descriptions, separate the surface compliment from the actual judgment underneath it.

questions.question_text:
The following text is from a novel. The narrator is describing a university administrator.
Professor Halden entered with the gravity of a portrait stepping out of its frame, and his expression implied a life spent issuing wise decrees. Yet one searched in vain for evidence of what his twenty-five years in university leadership had concretely produced. His recently published reminiscences promise illumination, but because they postpone the relevant details almost indefinitely, they preserve the mystery better than they solve it.
Based on the text, which choice best describes Professor Halden?

choices.A: He looks like a person of considerable distinction, but it is unclear whether his record actually justifies that impression.
choices.B: He appears respectable, but his memoir shows that several of his decisions were unethical.
choices.C: He would be more admired if his accomplishments did not need to remain hidden from readers.
choices.D: He has kept a deliberately low profile despite a long and evidently successful career.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: He would be more admired if his accomplishments did not need to remain hidden from readers.

correct_answer.text: He looks like a person of considerable distinction, but it is unclear whether his record actually justifies that impression.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: The narrator presents Halden as looking important while implying that his achievements are frustratingly hard to identify. That is exactly the contrast in choice A.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: The passage does not suggest hidden achievements; it suggests that the promised evidence of achievement never really arrives.
answer_explanation.takeaway: When a passage questions whether appearances are earned, the correct answer will usually preserve that uncertainty rather than invent a hidden explanation.

questions.question_text:
Studies of ocean wave breaking have predominantly focused on traveling waves (those propagating along the
horizontal plane), so Mark McAllister et al. utilized a circular wave tank to produce and study spike waves,
axisymmetric standing waves that can erupt vertically when traveling waves propagating in opposing directions
intersect. Traveling waves break when wave steepness (height-to-length ratio) passes a critical threshold; breaking
thus constrains wave height. McAllister et al. found that spike waves can exceed that constraint, as other factors than
just steepness (e.g., jet stability and cavity shape) mediate spike-wave breaking.
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

choices.A: Previous studies have suggested that steepness mediates breaking in traveling waves, but the study by McAllister et al. shows that jet stability and cavity shape may also influence breaking in such waves.
choices.B: The process of breaking limits the height of traveling waves, but the study by McAllister et al. suggests that spike waves can exceed those limits if their height-to-length ratio reaches a critical threshold.
choices.C: McAllister et al. suggest that spike waves can form when traveling waves propagating in opposing directions intersect and that spike waves tend to be higher than traveling waves.
choices.D: The study by McAllister et al. suggests that when traveling waves intersect in specific ways, the resulting wave may be higher than would be expected based on the properties of traveling waves.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Previous studies have suggested that steepness mediates breaking in traveling waves, but the study by McAllister et al. shows that jet stability and cavity shape may also influence breaking in such waves.

correct_answer.text: The study by McAllister et al. suggests that when traveling waves intersect in specific ways, the resulting wave may be higher than would be expected based on the properties of traveling waves.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: The passage explains that spike waves form when opposing traveling waves intersect and that they can exceed the height limits predicted from ordinary traveling-wave breaking. Choice D captures both the setup and the key conclusion.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A misstates the study by saying jet stability and cavity shape influence breaking in traveling waves. The passage says those factors help explain spike-wave breaking, which is the special case being studied.
answer_explanation.takeaway: For main-idea questions, make sure the answer stays on the passage's actual subject rather than drifting back to background information.

questions.question_text:
Studies of soap-film rupture have mainly focused on flat films stretched across frames, so Wen Li et al. investigated dome-shaped films that form briefly when bubbles merge. Flat films rupture when thinning passes a critical threshold; rupture therefore limits the size such films can maintain. Li et al. found that merged domes can exceed that limit because factors other than thickness alone, such as rim curvature and drainage pattern, affect when the domes burst.
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

choices.A: Previous studies have suggested that thickness mediates rupture in flat films, but the study by Li et al. shows that rim curvature and drainage pattern may also influence rupture in such flat films.
choices.B: Rupture limits the size of flat films, but the study by Li et al. suggests that merged domes can exceed those limits if their thickness reaches a critical threshold.
choices.C: Li et al. suggest that dome-shaped films form when bubbles merge and that such domes tend to be larger than flat films.
choices.D: The study by Li et al. suggests that when bubbles merge in specific ways, the resulting dome may persist at a size greater than would be expected based on the properties of flat films.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Previous studies have suggested that thickness mediates rupture in flat films, but the study by Li et al. shows that rim curvature and drainage pattern may also influence rupture in such flat films.

correct_answer.text: The study by Li et al. suggests that when bubbles merge in specific ways, the resulting dome may persist at a size greater than would be expected based on the properties of flat films.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: The passage introduces a special case, merged domes, and says they can exceed the limits predicted from flat-film behavior. Choice D captures that core idea.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A wrongly transfers the new factors back onto ordinary flat films. The passage says those factors mediate rupture in the dome case being studied.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Keep track of the background case and the new case. The main idea usually centers on what is newly learned about the new case.

questions.question_text:
Research on snow collapse has mostly examined level snow slabs resting on mountain slopes, so Marta Jensen et al. studied arching ridges that form when opposing drifts press into one another. Ordinary slabs fail when stress exceeds a critical threshold, which constrains how much snow they can support. Jensen et al. found that these ridges can exceed that expectation because factors other than stress alone, including curvature and wind packing, help determine when collapse occurs.
Which choice best states the main idea of the text?

choices.A: Previous studies have suggested that stress mediates failure in ordinary snow slabs, but the study by Jensen et al. shows that curvature and wind packing may also influence failure in such ordinary slabs.
choices.B: Collapse limits the weight ordinary snow slabs can support, but the study by Jensen et al. suggests that ridges can exceed those limits only when stress reaches a critical threshold.
choices.C: Jensen et al. suggest that arching ridges form when opposing drifts meet and that such ridges generally support more snow than ordinary slabs do.
choices.D: The study by Jensen et al. suggests that when opposing drifts create arching ridges, the resulting structure may support more snow than would be expected based on the behavior of ordinary slabs.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: Previous studies have suggested that stress mediates failure in ordinary snow slabs, but the study by Jensen et al. shows that curvature and wind packing may also influence failure in such ordinary slabs.

correct_answer.text: The study by Jensen et al. suggests that when opposing drifts create arching ridges, the resulting structure may support more snow than would be expected based on the behavior of ordinary slabs.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: The main point is that a special structure formed by opposing drifts can outperform what ordinary slab models would predict. Choice D says that directly.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A incorrectly makes curvature and wind packing sound like new findings about ordinary slabs rather than about the arching ridges.
answer_explanation.takeaway: A common trap is to misapply the new finding to the background phenomenon instead of the special case the study investigates.

questions.question_text:
Many music streaming services also function as social media platforms: by giving users the option to follow friends
and share curated playlists or information about their listening history, these platforms allow people to convey their
music preferences and listening activities directly to their social networks. In a 2023 study, researcher Michael James
Walsh interviewed frequent users of a popular music streaming platform to investigate how its social media
dimensions shape their listening habits. Walsh found that these dimensions tended to make study participants feel
more mindful of how their listening activities may be perceived, which in turn influenced how they managed those
activities.
Which quotation from a music streaming platform user would best illustrate Walsh's finding?

choices.A: "When I create playlists or choose songs to listen to, I often pick songs that I think the friends who are following me might be interested in listening to, rather than what I might actually prefer listening to in that moment."
choices.B: "Because the predictions the platform makes about what new songs I might like depend on my past listening habits, at times its recommendations can feel overly repetitive and unvarying."
choices.C: "The social aspects of music streaming make it really easy to create a sense of a shared listening experience with my friends, even if we aren't in the same room."
choices.D: "Listening to music through a streaming platform feels a lot more passive, since I don't need to make any deliberate choices about what music I'm going to consume, like I do when buying digital downloads or records and CDs."

selected_incorrect_answer.text: "Because the predictions the platform makes about what new songs I might like depend on my past listening habits, at times its recommendations can feel overly repetitive and unvarying."

correct_answer.text: "When I create playlists or choose songs to listen to, I often pick songs that I think the friends who are following me might be interested in listening to, rather than what I might actually prefer listening to in that moment."

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: Walsh found that social features make users conscious of how others may judge their listening activity, and that this changes what they do. Choice A directly shows that kind of self-management: the user picks music based on followers' reactions rather than personal preference.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice B is about an algorithm producing repetitive recommendations. It does not involve social perception or users changing their behavior because other people can see it.
answer_explanation.takeaway: To illustrate a research finding, pick the example that mirrors both parts of the finding, not just one related topic from the passage.

questions.question_text:
Many photo-sharing platforms also function as social networks: users can follow one another, react to posts, and publicly track each other's activity. In a recent study, researcher Alana Brooks interviewed frequent users to examine how these social features shape posting habits. Brooks found that the features often made participants more conscious of how their activity might be judged, which in turn influenced what and how they posted.
Which quotation from a photo-sharing platform user would best illustrate Brooks's finding?

choices.A: "When I decide which photos to post, I sometimes choose the ones that will make my feed look more impressive to followers rather than the ones I personally like best."
choices.B: "Because the app recommends images based on what I have already liked, the suggestions can start to feel repetitive after a while."
choices.C: "The social side of the app makes it easy to feel connected to friends even when we are not in the same place."
choices.D: "Using the app feels passive sometimes, because I can scroll for a long time without making any deliberate choices at all."

selected_incorrect_answer.text: "Because the app recommends images based on what I have already liked, the suggestions can start to feel repetitive after a while."

correct_answer.text: "When I decide which photos to post, I sometimes choose the ones that will make my feed look more impressive to followers rather than the ones I personally like best."

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: Choice A shows the user changing posting behavior because followers might judge the feed. That directly illustrates social perception shaping behavior.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice B is about recommendation algorithms, not about the user managing behavior because other people can see it.
answer_explanation.takeaway: The best illustrative quote should match both the social-awareness part of the finding and the behavior-change part.

questions.question_text:
Some fitness-tracking apps also operate like social platforms: users can follow friends, compare workout logs, and react to each other's activity. Researcher Devin Shah interviewed frequent users of one such app and found that its social features often made participants more mindful of how their routines would appear to others, which influenced how they recorded and even selected workouts.
Which quotation from a fitness-app user would best illustrate Shah's finding?

choices.A: "I sometimes log a longer run instead of a short walk because I know my followers will see the entry, and I want the workout list on my profile to look more serious."
choices.B: "The app's training suggestions can get repetitive because they are based so heavily on my previous workouts."
choices.C: "The social features make it easier to feel like I am exercising alongside friends even when we are in different cities."
choices.D: "Using the app can feel passive when I just accept the workout plan it gives me instead of planning one myself."

selected_incorrect_answer.text: "The app's training suggestions can get repetitive because they are based so heavily on my previous workouts."

correct_answer.text: "I sometimes log a longer run instead of a short walk because I know my followers will see the entry, and I want the workout list on my profile to look more serious."

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: inference, main idea, and evidence application

answer_explanation.why_correct: Choice A shows awareness of others' judgments shaping both what the user logs and what workout the user chooses. That mirrors the finding precisely.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice B concerns algorithmic repetition, not social visibility or self-presentation.
answer_explanation.takeaway: For evidence-application questions, the right example mirrors the mechanism in the study, not just the general topic.

questions.question_text:
Properties of TRAPPIST-1 Exoplanets Compared to Earth's
Properties
Planet | Orbital period (days) | Planet radius (Earth radii)
Earth | 365.26 | 1
TRAPPIST-1b | 1.51 | 1.09
TRAPPIST-1d | 4.05 | 0.77
TRAPPIST-1e | 6.1 | 0.92
TRAPPIST-1f | 9.21 | 1.04

TRAPPIST-1 is a planetary system with a central red dwarf star and several Earth-sized exoplanets. Each planet in this system is so close in orbit to the star that the gravitational forces acting between the planet and the star cause the planet's rotational period around its axis and orbital period around the star to be equal. For example, the orbital period of TRAPPIST-1e is ____.
Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the example?

choices.A: 1.51 days, which is the shortest rotational period of any of the TRAPPIST planets.
choices.B: 6.1 days, and its rotational period is also 6.1 days.
choices.C: 9.21 days, which is the same as the rotational period of TRAPPIST-1f.
choices.D: 6.1 days, and its rotational period is 0.92 days.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: 1.51 days, which is the shortest rotational period of any of the TRAPPIST planets.

correct_answer.text: 6.1 days, and its rotational period is also 6.1 days.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: tables, charts, and quantitative comparison

answer_explanation.why_correct: The table lists TRAPPIST-1e's orbital period as 6.1 days. The passage also states that each planet's rotational period equals its orbital period, so 6.1 days for both is the only fully supported completion.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A uses TRAPPIST-1b's orbital period, 1.51 days, instead of TRAPPIST-1e's. It answers the example with the wrong planet's data.
answer_explanation.takeaway: In table questions, confirm that the row, column, and stated relationship all match before choosing.

questions.question_text:
Properties of Lumen's Moons Compared to Earth's Moon
Properties
Moon | Orbital period (days) | Moon radius (Earth-moon radii)
Earth's Moon | 27.3 | 1
Lumen-b | 1.8 | 0.42

Lumen is a fictional planet with several moons that are tidally locked, meaning each moon's rotational period equals its orbital period. For example, the orbital period of Lumen-d is ___.
Which choice most effectively uses data from the table to complete the example?

choices.A: 1.8 days, which is the shortest rotational period of any of Lumen's moons.
choices.B: 5.2 days, and its rotational period is also 5.2 days.
choices.C: 8.4 days, which is the same as the rotational period of Lumen-e.
choices.D: 5.2 days, and its rotational period is 0.91 days.

selected_incorrect_answer.text: 1.8 days, which is the shortest rotational period of any of Lumen's moons.

correct_answer.text: 5.2 days, and its rotational period is also 5.2 days.

questions.study_category_label: Information and Ideas: tables, charts, and quantitative comparison

answer_explanation.why_correct: The table gives Lumen-d an orbital period of 5.2 days, and the passage says a tidally locked moon has the same rotational period. Choice B matches both facts.
answer_explanation.why_selected_answer_is_wrong: Choice A uses Lumen-b's value, 1.8 days, instead of Lumen-d's.
answer_explanation.takeaway: Verify the exact row first, then apply the stated relationship. Many trap answers swap in a nearby row.

questions.question_text:
Properties of the Nysa System's Satellites
Properties
Satellite | Orbital period (days) | Satellite radius (Earth-moon radii)

choices.C: 9.5 days, which is the same as the rotational period of Nysa-d.
choices.D: 6.7 days, and its rotational period is 0.83 days.
